audit_items.proof_trace: In Start(), if _maintain_thread_group->start() fails, the error block resets _maintain_thread_group to null (line 93) but omits 'return false', allowing execution to fall through. At line 156, _maintain_thread_group->io_service() dereferences the null shared_ptr, causing a crash. Secondary: ResetOptions() dereferences _flow_controller (lines 245/250) with no is-running guard, so calling it before Start() or after Stop() also NPDs.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker who can exhaust system thread resources (e.g., via connection flooding causing thread-limit exhaustion) during a server Start() or Stop()+Start() cycle could force _maintain_thread_group->start() to fail; the missing return false then causes the null deref at line 156, crashing the server process.
